examples: There are several options to deliver the cytosine gene.
Researchers at purdue university used synthetic forms of guanine and cytosine.
The team looked at methylation, in which a methyl group is placed onto cytosine in DNA sequences.
The four bases - adenine (A), guanine (G), thymine (T), and cytosine (C) - spell out the genes.
These symbols represent the four basic chemical letters, or bases, the body uses to form DNA--guanine, cytosine, adenine and thymine.
